title: Silvia serpyllifolia
taxonomy: Silvia serpyllifolia is actually known as Thymus serpyllum, but another common name is Thymus serpyllum's lookalike, Silvia serpyllifolia is not the correct name. The correct classification for the plant you are referring to is a type of thyme, specifically within the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Thymus and is native to Europe. Thymus serpyllum is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with tiny pink or white flowers. This plant is often used in herbal medicine and is also a popular ingredient in cooking.
uses: Silvia serpyllifolia, also known as Thyme-leaved Speedwell, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are said to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various ailments. Silvia serpyllifolia is often used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects. Additionally, it may be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Silvia serpyllifolia for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Silvia serpyllifolia, also known as Thyme Leaved Speedwell, is considered a non-toxic plant and is not typically associated with adverse side effects when used in small quantities.
